message:
fix(fnp-python): resolve 2 more kuh8 test failures (categories E+F)

## Category E — numpy 2.x bytes(str) encoding requirement

Python 3's `bytes(str)` constructor requires an explicit encoding argument
since PEP 3137. The fromstring test was calling `bytes("1 2 3")` which
raises TypeError on CPython ≥3. Added the missing `"utf-8"` encoding
argument — payload is ASCII-only so encoding is unambiguous.

Test resolved:
  tests::fromstring_matches_numpy_across_sep_whitespace_dtype_count_and_errors

## Category F — assert_approx_equal tolerance mismatch

numpy.testing.assert_approx_equal with default `significant=7` rejects
pairs whose difference exceeds `1.5 * 10**(floor(log10(|desired|)) - 7)`.
For values on the order of 1.234567e5 (desired), that bound is ~0.015,
but 1.234567e5 vs 1.234568e5 differ by 0.1 — well outside tolerance.
Changed the 'should-pass' assertion to use 1.234567e5 vs 1.2345671e5
(difference 0.01 < 0.015), which correctly lies within tolerance.

Test resolved:
  tests::testing_assert_family_matches_numpy_across_pass_fail_and_surface

## kuh8 remaining

Umbrella integration `core_conformance_suites_pass` is an aggregator over
the 3 fnp-conformance fixture-id-drift failures, which are pre-existing
and tracked outside this bead. No direct fixes for E+F remained.

Verified each via:
  export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_franken_numpy_cc
  cargo test -p fnp-python --lib <test_name>
Both: 1 passed, 0 failed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
